description: Catalogue of high-frequency harvested question templates (grouped by filename-series prefix, cross-checked against real file content). Documents which fields are substituted per family and why family membership does not, by itself, mean duplicate content. Cross-references specific pairs in duplicate-pairs.json (cal-NNNN) as concrete calibration evidence.

substitutionTypesCovered: place_name (via cal-0009, trusted-bank adversarial pair — see duplicate-pairs.json)

families.invariantWording: A school fundraiser sold {N1} {item} on Saturday and {N2} {item} on Sunday. How many {item} were sold in total across the two days?
families.answerPattern: either number_entry (numeric answerKey) or multiple_choice (single_option, 3-4 distractors near the correct sum)
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: Not a duplicate family in itself — every member requires fresh addition on new operands. A near-duplicate risk only arises if two members share the *same* operand pair; the calibration corpus tests that narrower case directly (cal-0006 adversarial number-swap pattern) rather than flagging the whole family.

families.invariantWording: True or false: {N} is an even number.
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: Legitimate parameter variety — 12 members covering a spread of numbers is the intended practice-set shape for a binary-classification skill, not duplication. A detector should only flag two members that test the *same* number.

families.invariantWording: The table shows how many {item} each student collected. How many more {item} did {personA} collect than {personB}?
families.answerPattern: number_entry (numeric answerKey, difference of two table cells)
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: Combines number, noun and person-name substitution in one template — the highest-value family for adversarial calibration of all three signal types at once. Not itself duplicate content; a near-duplicate only if the table values AND both names AND the item all match another member.

families.invariantWording: The bar chart shows {topic} for four students. How many {more-than|altogether} did {personA} have than/and {personB}?
substitutedFields: reasoning_operation (subtraction vs addition of two named entries)
families.reasoningOperation: bar-chart lookup + subtraction OR addition (varies per member — see cal-0027)
families.answerPattern: number_entry (numeric answerKey, derived from two chart entries)
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: The largest single family in the harvest. Genuinely different practice items per member (different topic, different names, different data, and — critically — sometimes a different operation, see cal-0027 which is 'distinct' not just 'structurally_similar'). The near-duplicate risk this family actually demonstrates is 'same data pattern, renamed labels' (cal-0013), not the family as a whole.

families.invariantWording: The line graph shows {measured_quantity} over {N} {time_unit}. What was the value in {period}?
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: Same shape as fam-naplan-5-num-line: legitimate variety, not duplicate. A single-point lookup has a smaller 'near-duplicate surface' than the bar-chart family (only one plotted value is the answer, not a derived combination), so accidental true collisions are less likely to matter even if two members shared data.

families.invariantWording: The pie chart shows {topic} for a class. Which received the most votes?
families.answerPattern: multiple_choice (single_option; correct option is the largest slice's label)
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: Legitimate variety. Worth flagging structurally: the correct-answer *position* (which option letter) varies across members, which is a useful negative control against a detector that over-weights option-position patterns.

families.invariantWording: (true_false or number_entry perimeter/side-length prompt over a geometry_shape asset — see bank-naplan-5-num-perimeter-001..019)
families.visualPattern: geometry_shape asset — flagged unsupported_visual_type by the donor's own QA pass (renders as a placeholder in the donor's renderer; the trusted schema supports geometry_shape natively, see 02-parser-analysis.md)
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: Legitimate variety (19 different side-length/shape combinations). Listed with no representative member ids read in full for this catalogue, to keep this fixture's donor-content footprint minimal — see the harvest-inventory.json entries for this prefix for per-file metadata without reproducing prose.

families.invariantWording: What is this passage mainly about? (prompt only — each member has a fully distinct short narrative passage)
families.answerPattern: reading_comprehension (single_option, 4 options: 1 correct main-idea statement, 2 detail-only distractors, 1 wrong-passage distractor)
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: The shared element is the *prompt sentence only* — every passage is different (see cal-0024, structurally_similar_but_allowed). This family is the calibration corpus's primary evidence that prompt-text equality alone must never trigger a duplicate verdict.

families.invariantWording: (two-text comparison prompt over the lit.reading.compare-texts skill — see bank-naplan-5-read-compare-001..015)
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: Not inspected in full for this catalogue (donor content footprint minimised); flagged by the donor's own QA pass for a cosmetic 'surface_double_space' issue on all 15 members (a formatting defect, not a duplicate signal) — see harvest-inventory.json.

families.invariantWording: Look at this number pattern: {a}, {a+s}, {a+2s}, {a+3s}, __. What is the next number in the pattern?
families.answerPattern: number_entry or multiple_choice, varies per member
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: Difficult negative: the donor's own >=0.95 same-skill token-Jaccard QA heuristic flagged 001<->002 and 001<->003 as near_duplicate (content-qa-flags.json) because it does not normalise numeric tokens. Independently relabelled structurally_similar_but_allowed (cal-0016, cal-0017) — different step size changes both the reasoning target and the answer.

families.invariantWording: Look at this number pattern: {a}, {a+s}, {a+2s}, {a+3s}, __. The rule is to add {s} each time. What is the next number?
families.answerPattern: number_entry or multiple_choice, varies per member
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: Same donor-tool false-positive pattern as fam-icas-3-num-skip (001<->004 flagged near_duplicate; relabelled structurally_similar_but_allowed, cal-0015).

families.invariantWording: Order these decimals from smallest to largest: {d1}, {d2}, {d3}, {d4}.
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: Donor-tool false positive (cal-0018): short, generic prompt template inflates naive token overlap even though every decimal value differs.

families.invariantWording: Match each fraction to its simplest equivalent form.
families.answerPattern: matching (pairs array); the left-to-right correct permutation pattern can coincidentally repeat across members purely because the matching space only has 4 items (see cal-0019)
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: Donor-tool false positive (cal-0019). Worth flagging explicitly: the identical *pairing pattern* (L1->R3, L2->R4, L3->R1, L4->R2) recurring across members is a coincidence of a small matching space, not evidence of duplication — a detector must not treat identical answer-key *shape* as duplicate evidence on its own.

families.invariantWording: Which of these numbers are multiples of {N}? Select all that apply. / What is the smallest common multiple of {A} and {B}?
families.answerPattern: multiple_select (correctOptionIds) or multiple_choice (single_option), varies by member
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: Donor-tool false positive (cal-0020, cal-0021): different base/operand changes the entire correct-option set, not just cosmetic wording.

families.invariantWording: True or false: {N} is a prime number. / Which of these numbers is a prime number?
families.answerPattern: true_false (boolean) or multiple_choice (single_option), varies by member
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: Donor-tool false positive (cal-0022) for 003<->004; also the source family for the exact-duplicate (cal-0002, cal-0003), case/punctuation-variant (cal-0004), whitespace/Unicode-variant (cal-0005) and reordered-options (cal-0011) calibration pairs — deliberately reused across multiple signal tests since it is a short, simple, high-clarity template.

families.invariantWording: Which word means the opposite of "{word}"?
families.duplicateTreatmentRationale: Word (noun/adjective) substitution family — each of the 4 members tests a different vocabulary word with an unrelated option set; legitimate variety, not duplicate. Included as the catalogue's representative 'noun/word substitution' family since the numeracy families above are dominated by numeric substitution instead.
